title: Safety chapter purpose and operator responsibility
summary: The safety chapter states that improper conveyor operation can cause serious injury, death, and equipment damage, and that the manual provides safe operating guidelines aligned with accepted industry safety standards.
details: The manual states that a carelessly and improperly operated conveyor can cause serious injury or death, as well as damage or destruction of equipment. It explains that the safety procedures in the manual are intended to provide safe operating guidelines and follow accepted industry safety standards. The text also states that users must read and understand the manual before operating the conveyor and ancillary equipment. Responsibility for compliance with local, state, federal, environmental, and site-specific safety requirements is assigned to the user.

Users must read and understand the manual before operating conveyor and ancillary equipment and must comply with local, state, federal, environmental, and site-specific safety standards.

title: Safety signal word definitions
summary: The manual defines the meanings of the signal words Danger, Warning, Caution, and Notice for communicating hazard severity and non-hazard important information.
details: Danger indicates a hazardous situation which, if not avoided, will result in death or serious injury. Warning indicates a hazardous situation which, if not avoided, could result in death or serious injury. Caution indicates a hazardous situation which, if not avoided, could result in minor or moderate injury. Notice indicates information considered important but not hazard-related, such as property damage or equipment damage, and the safety alert symbol is not used with Notice.

title: Tote handling and WCS coordination concept
summary: During normal operation, parcels are directed into totes until a configured tote limit is reached, after which the WCS coordinates sorter diversion control and AGV tote exchange.
details: The source states that once the sorter starts, parcels are directed into totes. When a tote reaches the customer-specified weight, parcel count, or volume, the WCS sends a message to the sorter PLC to stop diverting packages. An AGV is triggered through the WCS controller to retrieve the full tote, and another AGV places an empty tote in its place. The WCS then sends a message to the sorter PLC to resume sortation.

An AGV is triggered via the WCS controller to retrieve the tote. Another AGV places an empty tote where the full tote was removed.

title: Tipper and bagging flow reference
summary: QR-guided AGVs deliver totes to open tippers, where the tipper gripper pulls in the tote and deposits parcels into a parcel bag through a chute before the empty tote is removed.
details: The manual explains that floor QR codes direct the AGV carrying a tote to an open tipper. When the tipper gripper senses the tote, it pulls the tote onto the tipper. The tipper deposits the parcel into a parcel bag via a chute. A bag ID label is generated and placed on the bag by the bagging employee, and the bag is then placed on a take-away conveyor. After the tipping cycle is complete and the tote is empty, the WCS indicates to the AGV that the task is complete and the empty tote is removed.

title: Hospital station exception handling reference
summary: The hospital station is used for operator handling of exception totes such as mis-sorts, bad tips, or tote overloads, with AGV movement defined between the right and left sides of the station.
details: According to the source, in the event of a mis-sort, bad tip, or tote overload, the WCS directs the AGV to take the tote to the right side of the hospital station for operator handling. The AGV then moves to the left side of the hospital station to pick up an empty tote before re-entering the sorter area to await further instructions.

title: Tote exchange settings modes
summary: The Tote Exchange Settings interface supports three operating modes for tote exchange logic: Count, Cube, and Cube & Count.
details: The manual states that pressing the GET EXCHANGE SETTINGS button opens a pop-up screen with information related to totes in the system. title: Exchange settings threshold metrics
summary: The Exchange Settings pop-up exposes cube, count, dimension, and weight-related parameters used to describe tote exchange and chute closure settings.

Cube Threshold Pickup is the cube value at which an AGV can pick up a tote. Cube Threshold Priority is the cube value at which the exchange is prioritized. Cube Threshold Close is the cube value at which the chute should close. Count Threshold Pickup is the item count at which an AGV can pick up a tote. Length, Width, and Height are the dimensions of the tote and are not used in calculations. Maximum Weight is the total weight limit that triggers chute closure to prevent overloading. Weight Threshold is the weight value at which the chute should close.

title: Service access points and connected components for tipper replacement
summary: Tipper replacement may require access through the station side panel and involves disconnecting the control box, ClearLink Ethernet connection, air line, and conduit-fed services.
details: To access the motor, the station side panel may be removed by taking out nine self-tapping fasteners with a 3/8-inch wrench or socket. The control box is removed and the power cable disconnected at a 6-pin Molex connector, then pushed into the column. The Ethernet cable is unplugged from the ClearLink controller and pushed into the column. The air line is disconnected at the quick disconnect at the bottom of the column, with the quick disconnect remaining on the tipper side of the air line. The conduit carrying power, air, and Ethernet is then fully disconnected and the cables pulled free from the tipper.

title: Tipper commissioning overview
summary: Tipper commissioning is performed when a tipper component is replaced so the tipper can be aligned with the other tipper and the grippers and axes can be kept level.
details: The manual states that when any tipper component is replaced, that tipper needs alignment with the other tipper to ensure the grippers and axes are level.
